samples.xml_description:
<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU. The “a” and “b” are the two operands of the ALU, the “aluc” is the opcode, and the “r” gives out the result. “zero” means if the result is zero, “carry” means if there is a carry bit, “negative” means if the result is negative, “overflow” means if the computation is overflow, the “flag” is the result of SLT and SLTU instructions. The supported operations and corresponding opcode are shown below:
    parameter ADD = 6'b100000;
    parameter ADDU = 6'b100001;
    parameter SUB = 6'b100010;
    parameter SUBU = 6'b100011;
    parameter AND = 6'b100100;
    parameter OR = 6'b100101;
    parameter XOR = 6'b100110;
    parameter NOR = 6'b100111;
    parameter SLT = 6'b101010;
    parameter SLTU = 6'b101011;
    parameter SLL = 6'b000000;
    parameter SRL = 6'b000010;
    parameter SRA = 6'b000011;
    parameter SLLV = 6'b000100;
    parameter SRLV = 6'b000110;
    parameter SRAV = 6'b000111;
    parameter LUI = 6'b001111;
</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a">a 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b">a 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc">a 6-bit control signal for selecting the operation to be performed.</input>
        <output id="r">a 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">a 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">a 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">a 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">a 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">a 1-bit output representing a general flag, which is set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH" value="8"/>
        <parameter id="DEPTH" value="16"/>
        <parameter id="ADDR_WIDTH" value="$clog2(DEPTH)">Internal parameter.</parameter>
        <parameter id="ADD" value="6'b100000"/>;
        <parameter id="ADDU" value="6'b100001"/>;
        <parameter id="SUB" value="6'b100010"/>;
        <parameter id="SUBU" value="6'b100011"/>;
        <parameter id="AND" value="6'b100100"/>;
        <parameter id="OR" value="6'b100101"/>;
        <parameter id="XOR" value="6'b100110"/>;
        <parameter id="NOR" value="6'b100111"/>;
        <parameter id="SLT" value="6'b101010"/>;
        <parameter id="SLTU" value="6'b101011"/>;
        <parameter id="SLL" value="6'b000000"/>;
        <parameter id="SRL" value="6'b000010"/>;
        <parameter id="SRA" value="6'b000011"/>;
        <parameter id="SLLV" value="6'b000100"/>;
        <parameter id="SRLV" value="6'b000110"/>;
        <parameter id="SRAV" value="6'b000111"/>;
        <parameter id="LUI" value="6'b001111"/>;
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <description>
            Notice that below descriptions are along with tags <![CDATA[ <logic />]]>.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]>, must construct this logic as interal wire declaration. Or this <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]> can be declared as a `reg` but only be used in always(*) block.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic" />]]>, must construct this logic as `reg` declaration. Must update this logic, in an always block, based on other <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic" />]]> or <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]> states.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic_operation" />]]>, no declaration for this keyword. Just place this operation sequence in below mentioned description.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic_operation" />]]>, no declaration for this keyword. Just based on the keyword `assign` to construct description of this combinational logic operation.
        </description>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="zero">
        The output <![CDATA[ <logic refid="zero" />]]> flag is active if <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> is all 0.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="carry">
        The output <![CDATA[ <logic refid="carry" />]]> is equalled to the MSB of the <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> in cases <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="ADD" />]]>, <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="ADDU" />]]>, <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="SUB" />]]> , and <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="SUBU" />]]> alu opcodes. otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="carry" />]]> is 0.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="overflow">
        In case <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="ADD" />]]> ALU opcode, the output <![CDATA[ <logic refid="overflow" />]]> is active if (MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is deactive, MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> is deactive, and MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]> is active) or (MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is active, MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> is active, and MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]> is deactive). In case <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="SUB" />]]> ALU opcode, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="overflow" />]]> is active if (MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is active, MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> is deactive, and MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]> is deactive) or (MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is deactive, MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> is also deactive, and MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]> is active). Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="overflow" />]]> is 0.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="r">
        The output <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]> is assigned to the first 32 bits of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="res">
        The size of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> is 33 bits.
        <![CDATA[ <switch value="<input refid="aluc">"/>]]>
            <![CDATA[ <case values="<parameter refid="SLL" />, <parameter refid="SRL" />, or <parameter refid="SRA" />" />]]>
                <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> is result of appropriate shift operations between all 32-bit [0:31] <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> shifted by value of all 32-bit [0:31] <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]>, not just the first 5-bit of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]>.
            <![CDATA[ </case>]]>
            <![CDATA[ <case values="<parameter refid="SLLV" />, <parameter refid="SRLV" />, or <parameter refid="SRAV" />" />]]>
                <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> is result of appropriate shift operations between 32-bit <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]> and 5-bit <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]>.
            <![CDATA[ </case>]]>
            <![CDATA[ <case values="<parameter refid="ADD" />, <parameter refid="ADDU" />, <parameter refid="SUB" />, <parameter refid="SUBU" />, <parameter refid="AND" />, <parameter refid="OR" />" />, <parameter refid="XOR" />" />, <parameter refid="NOR" />" />, <parameter refid="SLT" />" />, <parameter refid="SLTU" />" />, or <parameter refid="LUT" />" />]]>
                <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> is the operation result normally from ADD, ADDU, SUB, SUBU, AND, OR, XOR, NOR, SLT, SLTU, and LUI.
            <![CDATA[ </case>]]>
        <![CDATA[ </switch>]]>
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="negative">
        <![CDATA[ <logic refid="negative" />]]> is set to the MSB of the output <![CDATA[ <logic refid="r" />]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="flag">
        In case of <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="SLT" />]]> opcode, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="flag" />]]> is active if 32-bit signed <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is less than 32-bit signed <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]>. In case of <![CDATA[ <parameter refid="SLTU" />]]> opcode, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="flag" />]]> is active if 32-bit unsigned <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> is less than 32-bit unsigned <![CDATA[ <input refid="b" />]]>. Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="flag" />]]> is set to 0.
        </logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>The module uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result <![CDATA[ <output refid="r" />]]> to the lower 32 bits [32:0] of the register <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]>. The <![CDATA[ <logic refid="flag" />]]> output is determined based on the control signal <![CDATA[ <input refid="aluc" />]]> and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and '0' otherwise. The <![CDATA[ <output refid="zero" />]]> output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal <![CDATA[ <input refid="aluc" />]]>. The result is assigned to the register <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res" />]]> accordingly. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of <![CDATA[ <input refid="a" />]]> are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal <![CDATA[ <input refid="aluc" />]]> does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as '0'.
Please provide correct `Logic description` fields above to achieve correctness for <![CDATA[ <module refid="alu" />]]> design in combinational logics.
</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. and the inputs are two 8-bit operands. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. res_valid must be constructed as combinational logic and not in any always block. So `res_valid` must not be declared as register.</output>
        <output id="result">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <logic_description>
        <description>
            Notice that below descriptions are along with tags <![CDATA[ <logic />]]>. These tags <![CDATA[ <logic />]]> could be described more detail in the below description in the <![CDATA[ <implementation />]]> tag.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]>, must construct this logic as interal wire declaration. Or this <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]> can be declared as a `reg` but only be used in always(*) block.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic" />]]>, must construct this logic as `reg` declaration. Must update this logic, in an always block, based on other <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic" />]]> or <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic" />]]> states.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="sequential_logic_operation" />]]>, place directly this operation sequence in below mentioned description.
            With a <![CDATA[ <logic type="combinational_logic_operation" />]]>, based on the keyword `assign` to construct description of this combinational logic operation.
        </description>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="divisor_extend">
        9-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="divisor_extend">]]> is set to concatenation of bit 1 and input <![CDATA[ <input refid="divisor">]]> if the input <![CDATA[ <input refid="sign">]]> and MSB of input <![CDATA[ <input refid="divisor">]]> is high. Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="divisor_extend">]]> is set to concatenation of bit 0 and input <![CDATA[ <logic refid="divisor">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic" id="NEG_DIVISOR">
        9-bit sequencial logic. Follow the below description in the <![CDATA[ <implementation />]]> tag for more detail in this sequential logic.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="all_sub_result">
        10-bit combinational logic. This is combinational, not sequential_logic.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="sub_result">
        9-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="sub_result">]]> is the first 9-bit of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="all_sub_result">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic_operation" id="sub_remainer_reg_divisor">
        10-bit combinational <![CDATA[ <logic refid="all_sub_result">]]> is set to summation of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="REMAINER">]]> and <![CDATA[ <logic refid="NEG_DIVISOR">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="REMAINER">
        8-bit combinational logic. This is a subsection of 8-bit MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic" id="SR">
        The 16-bit shift register.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="QUOTIENT">
        8-bit combinational logic. This is a subsection of 8-bit LSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="CO">
        1-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="CO">]]> is set to the MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="all_sub_result">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="mux_result">
        9-bit combinational logic. If <![CDATA[ <logic refid="CO">]]> is high, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="mux_result">]]> is set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="sub_result">]]> else set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="REMAINER">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="quotient_shift">
        7-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="quotient_shift">]]> is set to 7 bits [7:1] of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic_operation" id="SR_final_update">
        First, the 8-bit MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]> is set to 8-bit <![CDATA[ <logic refid="mux_result">]]>. Then bit [0:0] of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]> is set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="CO">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic_operation" id="SR_shifting">
        Concatenate <![CDATA[ <logic refid="SR">]]> from of first 7-bit of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="mux_result">]]>, 7-bit <![CDATA[ <logic refid="quotient_shift">]]>, 1-bit <![CDATA[ <logic refid="CO">]]>, and 1-bit zero.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="dividend_abs">
        8-bit combinational logic. If <![CDATA[ <input refid="sign">]]> and the MSB of input <![CDATA[ <input refid="dividend">]]> is high, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="dividend_abs">]]> is set to two's complement of the input <![CDATA[ <input refid="dividend">]]>. Otherwise <![CDATA[ <logic refid="dividend_abs">]]> is set to the original input <![CDATA[ <logic refid="dividend">]]> only.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="final_remainer">
        8-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="final_remainer">]]> is set to two's complement of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="REMAINER">]]> if the <![CDATA[ <input refid="sign">]]> bit and MSB of register <![CDATA[ <input refid="dividend">]]> is high. Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="final_remainer">]]> is set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="REMAINER">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="final_quotient">
        8-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="final_quotient">]]> is set to two's complement of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="QUOTIENT">]]> if <![CDATA[ <logic refid="is_final_quotient_sign">]]> is high. Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="final_remainer">]]> is set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="QUOTIENT">]]>.
        </logic>
        <logic type="combinational_logic" id="is_final_quotient_sign">
        1-bit combinational logic. <![CDATA[ <logic refid="is_final_quotient_sign">]]> is result of the `and` logic of <![CDATA[ <input refid="sign">]]> and the `xor` logic of MSB of <![CDATA[ <input refid="dividend">]]> register and MSB of <![CDATA[ <logic refid="divisor">]]> register.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic" id="start_cnt">
        1-bit sequential logic. When <![CDATA[ <input refid="rst">]]> is high, <![CDATA[ <input refid="start_cnt">]]> is reset to 0. Otherwise, follow the below description in the <![CDATA[ <implementation />]]> tag.
        </logic>
        <logic type="sequential_logic" id="res_valid">
        1-bit sequential logic.
        Must construc <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> in a separate always block executing when positive edge of <![CDATA[ <input refid="clk">]]> and positive edge of <![CDATA[ <input refid="rst">]]>.
        <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> is managed based on the <![CDATA[ <input refid="rst">]]> signal, the cnt, and whether the result has been consumed, which is signaled by the input <![CDATA[ <input refid="res_ready">]]>.
        <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> should be update directly, without passing middle register value.
        <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> is set to zero if <![CDATA[ <input refid="rst">]]> is high. 
        Otherwise, if `cnt` reaches 8, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> is set to 1. 
        Otherwise, if <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> and <![CDATA[ <input refid="res_ready">]]> is all high, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> is set to 0. Otherwise, <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]> is set to <![CDATA[ <logic refid="res_valid">]]>.
        </logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Operation Start:
When <![CDATA[ <input refid="opn_valid">]]> is high, <![CDATA[ <output refid="res_valid">]]> is low, and <![CDATA[ <input refid="start_cnt">]]> is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor in to 2 8-bit corresponding regisers.
Initializes the 16-bit shift register SR with the absolute value of the original input `dividend` (dividend_abs) shifted left by one bit.
9-bit NEG_DIVISOR is set to `divisor_extend`, if the sign bit and MSB of divisor is high. Otherwise, sets 9-bit NEG_DIVISOR to two's complement of the `divisor_extend`.
Sets the 4-bit counter cnt to 1 and <![CDATA[ <input refid="start_cnt">]]> to 1 to begin the division process.

Division Process(If <![CDATA[ <input refid="start_cnt">]]> is high, the module performs the following steps):
If most significant bit of cnt is set (the counter cnt reaches 8), the division is complete:
cnt and <![CDATA[ <input refid="start_cnt">]]> are cleared. Then, based on SR_final_update method, Updates the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.
Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register SR is updated based on the subtraction result `mux_result`:
Based on `sub_remainer_reg_divisor`, compute the summation of `NEG_DIVISOR` and `REMAINER`.
Uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out.
Based on `SR_shifting`, updates SR.

Result operation:
The output `result` is constructed as combinational logic and is set to concatenation of `final_remainer` and `final_quotient`. Notice that, because of combinational operation, the `result` must not be calculated in any always block.
</implementation>
</module>
